compiled_state.title: Skill tool returns 'Unknown skill: dso:fix-bug' after long session activity
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
The Skill tool returns 'Unknown skill: dso:fix-bug' when invoked late in a long session. The skill is registered and works at session start but becomes unavailable after extended activity (long context, multiple context compactions, or large tool-use counts).

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Run a long /dso:sprint session with multiple phases, context compactions, and sub-agent dispatches
2. After session close (/dso:end-session), attempt to invoke /dso:fix-bug via the Skill tool
3. Observe: 'Unknown skill: dso:fix-bug'

## Expected
Skill tool resolves dso:fix-bug regardless of session length or prior activity.

## Actual
Skill tool fails to resolve dso:fix-bug with 'Unknown skill' error. Other skills (e.g. /dso:end-session) invoked at the same point in the session resolve correctly.

## Hypothesis
Skill registry may be evicted or truncated during context compaction, or the available-skills list in the session context drifts as the context window fills. Skills invoked earlier in the session (before compaction) remain available; newly invoked skills after compaction may not.

comments.body: Additional data: problem persists after /reload-plugins (Reloaded: 1 plugin · 3 skills · 29 agents · 14 hooks). This rules out a stale skill registry from context compaction — the skill list is refreshed but dso:fix-bug still returns 'Unknown skill'. The system-reminder in this session shows dso:fix-bug IS present in the available skills list after reload, which suggests the Skill tool invocation is failing for a different reason than registry staleness. Possible cause: the skill's trigger condition (a bash check for the digital-service-orchestra plugin) is returning false despite the plugin being loaded.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Root cause hypothesis refined: the skill's availability check is a bash guard — 'timeout 3 claude plugin list 2>/dev/null | grep -q digital-service-orchestra'. If this subprocess call times out or produces unexpected output in a long session (e.g., due to resource pressure, the claude CLI being busy, or stdin/stdout contention), the grep fails and the skill is treated as unavailable. This would explain why /reload-plugins does not fix it — the plugin IS loaded, but the availability check fails at invocation time. Fix direction: either remove the bash guard from dso:fix-bug's trigger definition, replace it with a lighter-weight check, or increase the timeout.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Clarified registration structure: there are TWO registrations for this skill — (1) a qualified 'dso:fix-bug' entry that resolves correctly and appears in the skills list with a clean description, and (2) an unqualified 'fix-bug' wrapper stub with a bash guard trigger ('!bash -c timeout 3 claude plugin list...'). The 'Unknown skill: dso:fix-bug' error suggests the Skill tool is routing through the unqualified stub's bash guard rather than the qualified registration, and the guard is failing. Fix direction: investigate why Skill tool invocation of 'dso:fix-bug' hits the unqualified stub path instead of the direct qualified registration.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Update: /dso:fix-bug was successfully invoked in a subsequent session (after /reload-plugins). The skill resolved correctly and executed the full investigation+fix workflow on ticket c3f2-b72c, classifying it as llm-behavioral, dispatching dso:bot-psychologist, implementing the fix, passing review, and committing. This rules out a persistent skill resolution failure — the issue appears to be session-state-dependent (long session / prior context compactions) rather than a registration problem.
comments.author: Test
